businessSummary.oneLiner: The world's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and custom AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin hit a 2-year high of 20.1%. Key growth catalysts launch in earnest from April 2026 with Cybercab production and Optimus Gen 3.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services, charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg (23x) and auto sector avg (16x). Still extremely overvalued despite the selloff

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus of $2.53. Multiple compression pressure amid market correction

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit 2-year high of 20.1%. Margin recovery signal despite pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash to fund massive capex

keyMetrics.name: 2026 Capex
keyMetrics.interpretation: Aggressive expansion into Terafab, Cybercab factory. Over $20B planned

growthDrivers.title: Terafab semiconductor project officially launched March 21
growthDrivers.description: Terafab went live today. Musk warned chip supply won't be enough in 3-4 years. Tesla will produce AI5 chips in-house to reduce TSMC dependency and supply chips for FSD, Optimus, and robotics.

growthDrivers.title: $2.9B Chinese solar equipment purchase negotiations
growthDrivers.description: Reuters exclusive (3/20): Tesla negotiating $2.9B in solar manufacturing equipment from Chinese firms including Suzhou Maxwell Technologies. Targeting 100GW US solar production by 2028.

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 chip $16.5B foundry contract confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at AGM that Tesla AI6 (HW6) chips will be produced at its Taylor, Texas fab using 2nm (SF2P) process. $16.5B through 2033.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab production starting April, Macrohard AI venture
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI JV) is an enterprise AI platform diversifying revenue beyond auto.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb sales +35% YoY, Trump China visit rescheduled
growthDrivers.description: China-made Tesla EVs sold 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. White House announced Trump China visit to be rescheduled within 5-6 weeks.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader. FSD 8.2B miles of data, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration form the core moats. Samsung AI6 foundry deal accelerates custom chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion miles of FSD driving data. Overwhelmingly larger training dataset than competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (Terafab+Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2025: 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging

recentNews.headline: Terafab semiconductor project officially launches: Musk warns of chip shortage in 3-4 years
recentNews.significance: Tesla's in-house chip manufacturing project Terafab went live. Sawyer Merritt reported. Strategy to produce AI5 chips internally to eliminate chip supply bottlenecks.

recentNews.headline: CBS: Pentagon preparing ground forces for Iran deployment, market plunges
recentNews.significance: CBS reported detailed Pentagon preparations for Iran ground troops. S&P 500 fell below its 200-day MA for the first time in 200+ days, down 1.5%. 50,000+ troops in Middle East.

recentNews.headline: FSD Europe approval delayed again: Netherlands testing complete but pushed to April 10
recentNews.significance: Electrek reports vehicle testing complete but approval date slipped from March 20 to April 10. EU-wide approval expected by summer.

recentNews.headline: TSLA down 3.2%: Magnificent Seven momentum fades, shift to defensive sectors
recentNews.significance: Rotation from growth to defensive stocks. S&P 500 fell below 200-day MA for the first time, raising further downside concerns.

recentNews.headline: Reuters: Tesla negotiating $2.9B Chinese solar equipment purchase
recentNews.significance: Targeting 100GW US solar production. Accelerating energy business vertical integration.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 41-analyst consensus $406.84 (Hold). BNP Paribas predicts 68% crash ($120 target), BofA $460 (Buy), Wedbush $600 (Outperform). Upside expanded to 10.6% at current price.

risks.title: Iran ground force deployment: War escalation risk at peak
risks.description: CBS reported Pentagon's detailed preparations for Iran ground forces. Trump denied it but 50K+ troops are deployed in the Middle East. Oil surging, S&P 500 broke below 200-day MA.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD engineering analysis upgrade + Europe approval delayed again
risks.description: 3.2M vehicle probe upgraded to engineering analysis. 9 crashes (1 fatal) in limited visibility. Europe FSD delayed to April 10. Regulatory headwinds across the board.

risks.title: S&P 500 below 200-day MA: Market-wide technical breakdown
risks.description: First time in 200+ days S&P fell below 200-day MA. Rotation from growth to defensives accelerating.

risks.title: BYD competition accelerating
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M EVs in 2025. 640-mile sedan and 5-min charging narrowing the tech gap.

risks.title: Valuation burden at P/E 220x
risks.description: 10x premium vs S&P 500 avg. Multiple compression risk intensifying with rate cut expectations evaporating.

risks.title: $20B+ capex burden
risks.description: Massive simultaneous investments in Terafab, Cybercab factory, solar accelerating cash burn.

buyReasons.title: Terafab officially launched March 21: Custom chip era begins
buyReasons.rationale: Went live today. No other automaker can self-produce chips. Success completes the vertical integration of AI, FSD, and Optimus.

buyReasons.title: $2.9B solar equipment: Energy vertical integration
buyReasons.rationale: 100GW US solar production target. Self-supplied energy capability is a core competitive advantage in the data center power era.

buyReasons.title: RSI in deep oversold territory, 25% below highs
buyReasons.rationale: RSI at 28. Historically strong bounce levels. Down 25% from $489.88 highs.

buyReasons.title: China Jan-Feb +35%, Trump China visit rescheduled
buyReasons.rationale: China market rebound and renewed summit hopes for tariff relief.

overallOpinion: CBS reported Pentagon preparations for Iran ground forces, sending markets into panic. S&P 500 broke below its 200-day moving average, and Tesla dropped to $367.96, now 25% below its highs.
Terafab officially launched today (3/21) and the $2.9B solar equipment deal is progressing, but FSD Europe was delayed again to April 10 and the NHTSA probe remains unresolved.

RSI at 28 in deep oversold territory. Far below the 200-day MA ($392), offering significant bounce potential if macro stabilizes. But with Iran war risk at its peak, chasing the dip is risky. Wait and watch.

investmentVerdict.summary: The Iran ground troops report has pushed markets into panic mode. Terafab launch and solar investment are strong long-term catalysts, but war escalation and FSD regulatory headwinds create a toxic short-term mix. RSI 28 signals deep oversold but the geopolitical risk demands patience. Hold for existing holders, new entries should wait for clarity on Iran.

RSI 28, deep oversold. Significant bounce potential if macro stabilizes, but Iran risk is at peak. Hold. Target $406.84(+10.6%).

witty.hook: Opening a chip factory while the Pentagon opens a war front
witty.text:
$TSLA $367.96(-3.24%)

Musk launched Terafab today. The same day CBS reported Pentagon preparing for Iran ground troops. Timing is cruel.

Solar $2.9B, chips $16.5B, Cybercab April. Future cards are brilliant but the present is S&P below 200-day, RSI 28. P/E 220x growth stocks don't thrive in wartime.

Hold. When the war noise fades, Terafab will shine. Target $406.84(+10.6%).

empathy.hook: TSLA holders, 25% drop plus war fears. This is tough
empathy.text:
$TSLA $367.96(-3.24%)

$490 was just months ago. Now $368 with Iran war fears on top. It's natural to feel scared.

But the company launched Terafab today. Solar $2.9B and Cybercab April are on schedule. RSI 28 has historically been a strong bounce level.

Holding is the strategy now. When Iran risk clears, this is one of the first to bounce. Hold. Target $406.84(+10.6%).
